rows: Avalon Hotel and Bungalows Palm Springs

Website was not working properly, we had one free night stay through the Marriott Bonvoy Brilliant card (platinum status), but it wouldn’t let us book more than one night with the free night award.Anyways we called this hotel to ask if it was possible to keep the same room if we book two nights under two reservations. Not only did this hotel refuse to help us, but they also mentioned that they would charge us twice for pet fees since we had two reservations. Their website clearly says one time fee per stay.No point staying in a 400/night hotel if they try to nickel and dime you at every corner. You’ll probably find better service at a random motel than this place.

The best part of this hotel was the valet, albeit pricy. The front desk and hostess at peacock were alright. But pretty much everything else was lacking. Room wasn't cleaned on one night and when asking for towels because of this they were unfriendly.  The service at peacock was also bad. The coffee service in the morning had no pastries, no coffee, and no hot water...If this hotel was a book it would have a great cover and not much else. Not worth it.

I will update this when the day comes where I can get through to ANYONE when calling. I either get a merciless automated phone system that's impossible to get past, or I am put on hold. The sad thing is that I am calling about an event there as well as likely lodging for the same. I guess they don't need the business!
The lady bartender tonight charged me $5 for a Diet Coke can… the little drink gun has Diet Coke on it… so unnecessary

Pillows are too soft and standard rooms face an alleyway and dumpster.  Additional fees are a joke.  The pool is far too small and the cabanas have barely any amenities other than being super sweltering.

Though the room was ok, the dinner downstairs was SO noisy that we couldn’t hear our family across the table! Even though we went to another floor to have a nightcap, it was even more noisy!We checked out after the first night and moved to another hotel!

Perfectly nice hotel but not nearly as nice as four seasons and it costs more. AC doesn't work very well, no daily turn down service, impossible to get a person on the phone. It's a luxury hotel for people who have never stayed at an aman, rosewood, or the Austin four seasons.

If you are considering hosting your wedding event at the Austin Proper Hotel, we would strongly recommend not moving forward with this venue. We are deeply disappointment with the service provided for our wedding event hosted in September 2025. We had a horrendous experience working with our assigned event manager. We expected a seamless and memorable experience, but instead our experience with the event manager’s consistent lack of communication, lack of professionalism, and lack of punctuality is inexcusable for a hotel that prides itself on delivering luxury service.A significant issue was the lack of billing transparency. I was charged tens of thousands of dollars to my credit card without prior notice or an itemized statement. After I requested an itemized statement, multiple errors were found and a refund was not provided until after the wedding event. In addition to this, the event manager changed multiple line-item quantities and costs on the BEO without transparent communication and repeatedly pressured us to sign the BEO despite persistent errors across multiple drafts.The event manager’s disorganization was obvious throughout the entire planning process. When unable to answer our questions, he failed to admit gaps in his knowledge or provide timely follow-up. He would often provide us with false information. Rather than collaborating with us to reach solutions that made us feel understood and content, every obstacle we faced felt as though he was opposing our preferences and interests.Multiple issues arose on the day of our event that clearly demonstrated a lack of support and respect from the entire Austin Proper Hotel team. The staff berated our vendors during setup which is unprofessional, disrespectful, and created unnecessary tension. During buffet service, several items ran out and were not replenished promptly, negatively impacting the guest experience. In addition, two unauthorized individuals were permitted access to the event floor and crashed our event.We are deeply dissatisfied in the mismanagement of the event planning experience and substandard execution of several key aspects of our special day. We worked with 30 vendors across our multi-event wedding week, and the Austin Proper Hotel was the only vendor that added unnecessary anxiety, stress, and negativity to the process. We trusted the team to deliver exceptional service, and it was disheartening to encounter innumerable issues during an important occasion.

The Room rates do not tell the complete story. There are excessive charges for too many things.There are multiple tax fees (to be expected) but there is an Amenity Fee of $130. I was told it was for things like the spa, workout area, morning coffee area, etc. One would think that at ~$700.00/nt. Plus taxes and fees workout area might be included.Dinner was excellent and reasonable. Breakfast was poor and over the top expensive. A two egg omelette with a slice of toast and watered down coffee, with tip was $42.00 for one person

I may be jinxed but the marriott online reservation process always requires a phone call - yes, wasting 21 minutes on the phone. I manage the IT for a firm so the marriott reservation struggle is a mystery to me. I tried to reserve a room using the AAA rate. The reservation would not go through, hence the dreaded call. Despite my Bonvoy status, I am going to give Marriott a pass in the future.BTW - Austin hotel tax is one of the highest in the country at 17%.Expect to get dinged with fees.

There is a nightclub downstairs and the hotel’s designers apparently partied too hard and slept through the class on noise insulation, so you’re going to hear loud pounding music until 1am on weekends - fun if you’re at the club, sucks for everyone else.If you complain about it they’ll act super surprised to hear this news (despite the bass being audible everywhere in the first several floors) and offer to move you to a lower tier room farther from the club.Nice bathrooms though.

OverallNice hotel but beware of feesPositive+ Nicely designed hotel and rooms.+ Staff very friendly and helpful+ Well maintained and cleaned+ Breakfast tasted goodNegative- The restaurant added a 4% "Wellness fee" to all bills. Asking at the reception I was told this was to help the community. Researching only it seems it is meant to pay for employees' health benefits. Really poor style to add such costs as extras, instead of having them included in the base price of the dishes. This is also on top of the daily "Resort fee" being added to room cost.- My in-room speaker didn't work (cable didn't provide power). I mentioned it twice during my three day stay but it was never fixed.- While my room was cleaned well daily, stuff I had rearranged (lamps, hotel ads, etc.) were always moved back. I don't want to always have to look at ads for the spa while washing my hands.- I couldn't see any environmental work being done

Needs to get the basics right.1. My room was within a few feet of a parking garage. No sunlight and the view of a parked car staring at me every morning.2. Small room with poor layout. No drawers for clothes and minimal seating area. Awkward experience ordering in room dining with little place for the food to be set up.3. No laundry or dry cleaning on the weekend. The clothes are sent out and the service is not available on the weekend. Not suitable for a premium hotel.
Felt like a four star hotel, definitely not 5 star. The rooms were very small and basic, the staff had no idea what was going on and then lobby / restaurants were dirty, and disorganised.

Overall, I liked this hotel and had a positive stay, but there are some notable drawbacks worth mentioning. During busy times, the hotel feels overcrowded, elevator waits can be long, parking is difficult with the valet when it's busy, and the check-in area can get backed up as well.The lobby bar is very pretty but gets  busy on Friday and Saturday nights.Unfortunately, my Bonvoy Gold Elite status was not honored nor did I receive any benefits from it which was disappointing.That said, the rooms were cozy and comfortable, and aside from the capacity issues, I really don't have major complaints about the stay itself. It feels like the hotel could benefit from better crowd management or more space to handle peak times.Dining and amenities were strong highlights. In-room dining was excellent, and breakfast at The Peacock stood out with very flavorful, tasty dishes. I'll provide a separate feedback for it.The gym is one of the best I've seen at a hotel well equipped, including Pilates tables, a steam room, and overall great facilities. The massage spa was also excellent and very relaxing. pricing is reasonable for the massage at a high end hotel.Goldie's, the bar in the evening, and the tea and coffee spot in the morning (with pastries) were especially warm and welcoming - easily one of the highlights of the stay.I'd rate this closer to a 4-minus, mainly due to pricing which come with some expectations. In my humble opinion, the hotel is a bit overpriced for what it offers. I've stayed at higher-end properties, and this one feels slightly behind compared to others in that category.

Our stay was great.  The staff is friendly, the place is clean, and the food in all restaurants was tasty.  We especially enjoyed Ryan at the front desk, Dan as bellman, Makenna as hostess to La Pascina and Pedro as waiter.

I would return again to this hotel.  It's very fun and beautiful.  We enjoyed the restaurant and the small bar off the lobby.  The rooms are fantastic, clean and have all the necessities.  We had a couple hiccups that were more than adequately resolved.  I look forward to our next stay.  Room 304, a King room with sitting area, is great!
Early dinner at the Peacock which was great as always, love any one of the hummus. Ryan and Michael in valet were speedy and fantastic. Always a good time.
Beautiful design hotel. Great public spaces, restaurants and bars. Spa was ok. Good treatment but the cold plunge and sauna area very disappointing.
We always love the proper! The customer service is excellent, food is awesome, and the rooms are cozy. We come here for a getaway yearly.
I had an outstanding experience. The food was delicious and the setting was romantic and scenic.

This is truly one of the best hotels I have stayed in. The location, the design, the vibes, the in room amenities, the cleanliness - every single detail is thought of and executed flawlessly. The rooms are built so wonderfully that you truly can’t hear anything outside of your own room - very peaceful. Even more impressive was the amazing service we experienced during our stay. Every interaction with the staff was led with such kindness and thoughtfulness - it truly was so wonderful. My husband and I stayed here to celebrate our anniversary and made our way back the following month because we had such a wonderful time. The restaurants on site are all so incredibly delicious and a perfect way to spend an afternoon or evening. The spa is such a special treat and the gym is so great -  complete with actual Pilates reformer machines! They have truly thought of everything. Stay here if you get the chance - you won’t regret it!

We just stayed at the Austin Proper Hotel and I honestly can’t say enough great things about this place. From the moment we arrived, the experience felt elevated, thoughtful, and genuinely warm.A huge shout-out to Anis, the Director and GM of Experience, along with Elina, Kristin and the guest services team. Their hospitality was exceptional. They went above and beyond in every way, including a complimentary upgrade to a stunning King Premier Guest Suite and a beautiful arrival welcome with champagne and chocolates. They made us feel completely at home and treated like VIPs from start to finish.The hotel itself is absolutely gorgeous. The design strikes that perfect balance of hip, cool, elegant, zen, and spa-like. Every space feels intentional and calming, yet vibrant and full of energy.The wellness and fitness amenities are next level. Incredible gym, spa, steam room, dry sauna, cold plunge, infrared panels, and pools—all beautifully designed and immaculately maintained. It’s one of the best wellness setups I’ve experienced at any hotel, anywhere.Add in the great restaurants and overall vibe, and Austin Proper truly delivers a luxury experience that still feels relaxed, modern, and soulful—never stuffy or pretentious.This place sets the bar very high for hospitality. If you’re coming to Austin and want an unforgettable stay where you feel genuinely cared for, Austin Proper is it. We can’t wait to come back and we will be visiting frequently.   P.S.  Anis, Elina and Kristin all should be promoted with huge raises.  They are all role models for what 5 star service should be!
Good: Great hotel, fun bar, lovely room, friendly staff.  Moved me to a great room when asked.

Positives: Nice facility,  great food, service was great. Our restaurant server-Will and baggage claim helper-Hardwick were especially helpful.  They had a nice coffee/crepe place just across the bridge and on the other side of the street. I highly recommend it as well.Negatives: Ask for a room on a higher floor! Our room was on the 3rd Floor, and the music was Booming so loud from their club that it made it hard to go asleep. Granted it was a Friday night, but still unacceptable.  Also, the hotel location was surrounded by homeless folks both on the  streets and on the river walkway. Two of them were building a tent (which I'm glad they had some shelter) but it's just a forwarding if you plan on staying there and going outside. The river also had a bad smell.

I usually stay at the Proper when I’m in Austin.  Apart from the captivating ambiance and delicious food, the service is always top notch.  I rarely leave reviews but was asked to do so this time.  I have nothing but good things to say anyway!  Kendra, Stephen & Miguel were on it, from the moment I arrived to the moment I left this trip!  There was a mixup in my NYE celebration reservation, and Giulio was polite & quick to accommodate.
Great location, nice rooms, good restaurants/amenities. Restaurants/Bar can be over run with locals.

Great experience overall and easily one of the best hotels in Austin. Every food and beverage outlet was excellent, and the style and décor throughout the property are beautiful. A special shout-out to Dodd in the Quill Room for the lovely service and spot-on dinner recommendations. Everyone on property was genuinely friendly and welcoming. The only small miss was that the scheduled turndown service on night three did not happen, but it did not take away from an otherwise outstanding stay. I cannot wait to come back.
The best dinner at peacock and an incredible night at the quill - Giulio is the best host ever in this proper hotelRooms were amazing and the entire atmosphere was great
wonderful hotel with great restaurants and bars on the lower levels
This was one of the nicest stays in a long time. Absolutely beautiful hotel. Love the design details and whole vibe. Had a fantastic breakfast and incredible service. Wish our stay was longer!

My teen daughter and I had weekend trip to the hotel and had a wonderful stay. The hotel is a great city hotel - beautifully designed with really comfortable, decently sized rooms and in a good location.Warm reception, strong service through and through - front desk, bars, restaurants (we ate at both), valet. There is quite a scene in lobby bars which I had no problem with - it was a cool scene and I didn't hang out there but so folks know. Mexican and Med restaurants served great food.The weather was too cold to use the pool and the hotel is missing a hot tub. Fitness facility is solid.Nice coffee station in morning next to front desk (had a good drink in same room night before which was far more quiet than main lobby bar).Front of hotel was a serious wind tunnel and I asked if it was normal to be blown away entering and exiting the hotel or just weekend we were there and I was told it was pretty normal! No big deal but just FYI - hold on to your hats!
My wife and I loved staying at this hotel. Impecable service.  The restaurants are great.  We loved La Piscina. Highly recommend!

Love going here on a weekday to work when I have minimal meetings. The coffee is great, many areas to sit and the staff are easy going. Multiple options for coffee, food and alcoholic drinks (if you end up staying for happy hour). There’s parking on the street, valet and multiple garage options for a fee.
Kendra was the person who helped us with valet and she was so kind! She even gave us a recommendation for dessert at the restaurant we were going to in the hotel. Thank you,Kendra!

The level of service here is outstanding. I felt so at home and they were all so lovely and welcoming. They were also welcoming to my dog and we had a very memorable time together. The design here is not just interesting but it also makes you feel like you could just move in and stay forever. Thank you to the entire staff for the wonderful stay. It’s an oasis in the city, with beautiful trails and restaurants right outside.

Huge shoutout to Tyler at the front desk! I haven’t slept through the night in (maybe 6 months? More????) because….kids. She set me up with a great room  to relax in for a night and get some rest. This Mom is very grateful!!!!  Also, in general, this hotel is great. I always direct visitors in Austin to their restaurant.

I stayed at the Austin Proper for roughly a week while visiting family.  Hotel is kept in pristine condition, well-located and unique.  Plenty of great dining options on-property.  I also just experienced (Dec 2025)  their new Omakase restaurant - Kappo Kappo; a must-try for sure.Chloe at the front desk was very helpful with all my needs, including the initial reservation.  The entire front desk staff is always eager to help. Evie in the coffee lounge in the AM very kind, helpful, and always makes everyone feel welcomed.I would not hesitate to stay again and highly recommend this property.Alexander Lo
The lady bartender tonight charged me $5 for a Diet Coke can… the little drink gun has Diet Coke on it… so unnecessary

I stay here Everytime I visit Austin, easily the best spot in town. Giulio, Chris, and Marshall are the best and take care of us every time, love the lobby bar and goldy’s. Can’t say enough about them and the energy there.

This is a true 5-star hotel. We spent a week here so I can truly say that the entire staff is super helpful and friendly. All the restaurants are great. Housekeeping is amazing. Gym is large and well appointed. The Goldie’s hostess in the morning is a gem. I would go back again and again.

Pillows are too soft and standard rooms face an alleyway and dumpster.  Additional fees are a joke.  The pool is far too small and the cabanas have barely any amenities other than being super sweltering.

As a resident, I just want to give a huge shoutout to Evie! Over the past couple of years, she has transformed Goldie’s morning coffee bar into something truly special. The hotel's complimentary coffee and beautiful space are wonderful, but it’s Evie’s kindness and her ability to anticipate everyone’s needs that make the morning ritual unforgettable. We all love her!

As a hotel guest, we would have liked to eat breakfast here on Sunday, but it was completely booked. The hostess said she would take our name and never contacted us. It is ridiculous that hotel guests cannot walk downstairs for breakfast because no guests are made a priority.
Thank you for having so many gluten free options!
It was very crowded but we felt like we received good service.
The bar was a bit busy, but it did take quite a while to be served at our table during the afternoon. Our evening cocktails were provided very quick and
Smoothies were not available on my final day which was a bummer.
Brunch was very good. It would have been nice for guests to have an option to dine in at the restaurant on Friday or Saturday night even with a waitlist.
The Peacock is amazing! We had the best time and the food was so delicious. The service was sooooo great. We love it.

What I love about the Peacock is the hosts.  In the morning I'm usually a little tired and just need to grab my morning Latte (Iced Grande Non-Fat Extra Shot)....and I call from my room, admittedly in my PJs (haha), and ask to have the latte waiting for me at the host stand.  I give it 5 minutes, cruise down, pick up my waiting latte, sign the check and boom, back in my room in minutes.   This seems to be a daily, morning tradition for me.   I've had lunch here a few times in the past, not on this trip - but that cheese that is lit on fire and the egg dish is so good. (can't remember name...).

Limited main course selections that seemed unnecessarily complicated. Sometimes less in more, vis-a-vis the dishes themselves. Gave the impression of an attempt to sound like the dishes were more than they really were.

Our waiter, whose name I unfortunately cannot remember but he had curly hair and blue eyes- was wonderful. Very nice, attentive, accommodating, knowledgeable, warm. He was great. The meal itself was excellent as well.
Once the server found me, service was quick and good and drinks both great.

We had a poor experience at the peacock for one of our dinner reservations. Between the appetizer and entree was over an hour gap. We were no longer hungry because we filled up on pita bread. Disappointed.
There was an incident with the lights, but Alex took the effort to make it right.
You do not hold the hot tea right behind a toddler and try to drop off beside her. This is a huge risk

Our pancakes weren't ready until after we'd finished the home fries, bacon, eggs, coffee and green juice. We were full at that point and in a hurry to leave. Our waiter was kind enough to remove the charge.  My husband did taste them and said they were great but something needs to be done or a warning attached! There is no such thing as "dessert pancakes" for breakfast but it was a nice attempt on the waiters part. I left a nice tip to reward his creativity.

We ate breakfast at The Peacock every day ( we were there for 5 days). Even though the tables were empty, I had to wait if I didn't have a reservation. The servers were not very efficient. We had the same waiter (whose name I do not recall) who waited on us twice. He was very slow, did not bring all of our dishes, and was not very pleasant when we asked for them. I realize it is not helpful not knowing his name. Meanwhile, our experience with staff in every other area of the hotel was excellent.  We did not find this in this restaurant.

Dinner on NYE was wonderful, and our waitress was friendly and helpful. Lattes (in the morning) were watery and I don’t think the second one I ordered on 1/1 was made correctly. The waitress who took my latte order on 1/1 did not seem as friendly, but she could have been busy or overwhelmed. I’m a coffee lover, so I think better coffee drinks would elevate the morning experience. My ratings above are more reflective of my dinner experience at Peacock’s.
Amazing presentation and quality. A truly unique flare blending French and Japanese style. Home Run!! Congratulations to the brothers.

What a cool, fun, delicious experience!! We loved it and the vibes and food were so wonderful. Every detail was thought of - no surprise bc that’s a theme throughout this hotel - but it truly showed even at dinner.

Kappo Kappo was a truly special experience. Incredible food, staff and presentation.  I believe it is well on its way to a Michelin Star.  In fact, the next morning I told some friends to hurry up and try it while they still are able to get in!
Incredible experience, delicious and fun. I will be back!
Was great. Loved the button to push when you’re ready for the items to be picked up. Great idea.
I did not feel like the room service menu had enough variety. I would have liked some alternative options that were more generic to accomodate a quick snack vs. a full meal.

I’m a sucker for in room dining and this did not disappoint. In fact, this was the highlight for me as a mom of littles. All I wanted to do was relax in bed and this was the icing on the cake!! So delicious and fast and prompt and all  the things. I’m sad my stay is over!
The food was absolutely incredible. The shakshuka was amazing.
bread on the club sandwhich could have been toasted more - was a little mushy
I asekd for fruit and nuts, and it arrived perfectly!
There was a slight issue with the cookie that was resolved
I would say this experience was far too expensive with the added on fees and tip (which I’d like to determine myself but did not have a choice).
Great in-room dinner with friendly staff.
I ordered the in room services. The Fettuccine Alfredo and shrimp are very salty and I can't really eat it. I almost throw the whole thing away.

One note - I couldn't understand the person who took my drink order and they could barely understand me (I have a Southern accent). We both repeated ourselves over and over for clarity and the correct order was delivered, however, you may want to reconsider who is taking orders going forward.
I asked if tea was complimentary of what was charge and I was told complimentary and then received a bill.
Two issues: 1) Menu descriptions on the app do not align with the menu information.  2) food had a strange flavor from exposure to cleaning products in the kitchen.
When booking you state breakfast for 2 that can be used in room. Then upon arrival you are given a $60 credit. That is only enough for 1.  Seems false advertising.  Will contact Amex about this.
We got some juices and a plate of fries. We couldn't believe how good the fries were. Absolutely incredible.
In fairness, we are coffee snobs... this was not very tasty...LOVED that staff accommodated an earlier time to deliver the coffee early but it wasn't the best tasting.
Jacob the bartender and his partner at our event were awesome

The New Year’s Eve event in the Quill Room was fantastic. The atmosphere was vibrant, well-paced, and perfectly balanced between high energy and comfort. The snacks were delicious, and the overall vibe made it a great space to welcome the new year. Giulio played a key role in shaping the evening, and his friendly, approachable presence added a great deal to the experience. It was clear that the flow, tone, and energy of the room were intentional and well managed.

The bar was packed, but there were some reserved tables to the side for hotel guests.  Thank God for that as our reservation at Kappo Kappo was an hour away!  We recently had a similar experience at the Hotel Chelsea in NYC as the bar there was very popular with locals but they always had a table for guests.
We were seated outside on the 27th and then nobody came and took our order so after 15-20 minutes we left without ordering and just drank in our room on patio.
We did struggle to get service here.  Once we did, the drink was great.

Hard to fully review as this was used for a private event. The space was intimate and had a great vibe, but the acoustics were awful. I remember trying to speak at the top of my voice trying to mingle over cocktails. It wasn't the best experience.
Dodd was great. Warm and welcoming
Casey was fantastic to have as my bartender.
While the creativity of the food was nice but that didn't mean it's a good idea for everyone.  The options were limited. only ate breakfast once and it was the chicken sausage and turkey bacon.
This was probably my best food experience in the hotel. Beverages were great. Enjoyed Dulce’s DJ set on Friday night and the general vibe/atmosphere of the place.
Fun space.  Need higher end tequilas and ports and Sauternes
We ate and drank at the outdoor patio and it was charming.
Again place was full so had to sit outside with drinks
Menu is odd. Suggest adding some kind of upscale bar nuts or other “snack” like items that are simple and unfussy.
The pecan bites needed to be slighted reheated but staff got it done

guest was surprised that they did not receive turndown service and needed water

guest mentioned that her friend receives a reset and recharge amenity
guest came to pk host stand at 6:55 asking for two barstools from the pk bar to go to their room

GRA mixed up the room number and placed a ticket to strip + pick up tray to the wrong room. FOP was in the room sleeping and got disturbed

Shortly after 11pm guest called down inquiring about her request for towels she submitted around 10:30pm. Guest also stated that she did not receive HSPK service.

Due to MOD stepping away from desk completing deliveries, guest was calling front desk and had a long delay. During the phone call guest stated that each time he stays here, he spends a long time trying to get ahold of the front desk in the evening.
Guest req a 7am wake up call, Night MOD called the room at 7am but he did not get an answer. Guest texted at 8.20 reporting no one called her.Guest is very upset.
Room was said to check out yesterday & cleaned as a checkout yesterday. Room was checked out this AM and flipped to clean. Room is still occupied by guest. Glitching for visibility
guest advised there is no bluetooth speaker in the room via Canary

Guest let us know after checking in that the mini bar was not replenished and wanted to avoid any charges.

MOD worked closely with them to explore all possible options for starting the vehicle. MOD and Mrs. Childress agreed that the best course of action would be to contact a Mercedes dealership to resolve the key fob issue. Guest is in good spirits.

Mr.Condit James was checking in and the reservation was under Robinson megan. He was an add on her res. As i was going through the check in process, i mentioned to Mr. Condit if Megan will be joining since she is the primary person on the res  and also  i was going thru her ambassador status. Mr Condit became "eerie" and he asked me if am allowed to say her name out loud like that. I told him i do not see any notes or was i given an alias name to use am just going thru my check in process.

MOD apologized for the oversight in not parking the vehicle as requested and explained that the residue may have originated from a specific parking area in the garage, as no birds were observed in the drive at the time. Parking for one night was COMPED. The guest responded neutrally to the apology. MOD will follow up.

Guest checked in to 408 and was confused since the room did not look like the 15 series he stayed in last time.
Guest was checked in to 324 and came down saying the room smelled like garbage.
